movie.title: The Uprising
movie.overview: During the English Peasants' Revolt in 1381, a farmer becomes a leader of the rebellion against the Crown forces of a then-14-year-old King Richard II.

aiEnrichment.summary_one_sentence: During the 1381 English Peasants' Revolt, a farmer rises to lead the rebellion against the forces of 14-year-old King Richard II.
aiEnrichment.summary_short: The Uprising is a historical action drama set during the English Peasants' Revolt of 1381. A humble farmer is thrust into leadership as oppressed villagers rise against the Crown. The rebellion pits commoners against the royal forces of the young King Richard II. The film explores themes of justice, sacrifice, and the cost of defiance.
aiEnrichment.summary_long: In 1381, widespread discontent among English peasants erupts into open rebellion against heavy taxation and feudal oppression. A local farmer, weary of injustice, emerges as an unlikely leader among the revolt's ranks. As the uprising gains momentum, he organizes villagers and marches toward London to confront the Crown. Opposing them is the army of King Richard II, who is only fourteen years old and guided by older advisors. The farmer must navigate internal divisions among the rebels while facing brutal retaliation from royal forces. Skirmishes and sieges test the resolve of the commoners, many of whom have never wielded weapons before. Tensions rise as the rebellion threatens the stability of the realm and forces the king's council to respond. The farmer becomes a symbol of resistance, but also a target for those who wish to crush the revolt. As the conflict reaches its climax, loyalty and betrayal shape the outcome of the struggle. The film closes on the heavy toll of the rebellion and its place in history.
aiEnrichment.synopsis: The Uprising is a historical drama and war film depicting the English Peasants' Revolt of 1381. It follows a farmer who becomes a central figure in the rebellion against the Crown during the reign of the young King Richard II. Blending action and period drama, the story portrays the struggles of ordinary people rising against systemic oppression. The film captures the atmosphere of medieval England and the human stories behind a pivotal moment in history.

story_structure.climax: The rebellion reaches a decisive confrontation with the king's representatives, forcing a moment of reckoning between the peasants' demands and the power of the Crown.
story_structure.conflict: The peasantry's grievances against heavy taxation and unfair laws ignite open rebellion, placing the farmer in opposition to the Crown and its forces loyal to the young King Richard II.
story_structure.resolution: The uprising's fate is sealed through negotiation, betrayal, or suppression, leaving the farmer and the revolt transformed by their clash with the realm.
story_structure.introduction: A humble farmer in 1381 England lives under the burdens of feudal oppression, unaware that the simmering discontent of the peasantry will draw him into history.
story_structure.risingAction: The farmer emerges as a voice and organizer among the rebels, uniting villages and marching toward London as tensions with royal authority escalate.
